skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Create design-forward PNG and PDF artwork from a visual philosophy, with local font guidance and minimal text.
skill.summary: Create polished static artwork as PNG or PDF from a written visual philosophy.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the license and font-license hits are false positives from legal text and URLs. The meaningful residual issue is that SKILL.md instructs the assistant to download fonts when needed, which can cause network access outside the bundled assets. No evidence found of credential access, data exfiltration, destructive commands, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Network Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to download and use fonts when needed. This is not malicious by itself, but it can trigger external network access during design generation and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction directly requests downloading fonts, so network use is plausible. I found no evidence that downloaded content is used to exfiltrate data or execute commands.

low_findings.title: License URLs Flagged As Network Indicators
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs in Apache and SIL Open Font License text are attribution and license references. They do not perform network requests and are false positives for active networking behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are legal license references and copyright source links. They are static text, not executable code or outbound request logic.

low_findings.title: Legal Terms Misclassified As Dangerous Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts occur in ordinary license wording, such as author definitions, distribution terms, and warranty disclaimers. These are false positives and do not indicate cryptographic use or host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the flagged lines are standard Apache or SIL Open Font License clauses. No encryption APIs, hashes, host probes, or command execution are present in those lines.

low_findings.title: Markdown Directory Reference Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged inline backticks around a local font directory reference. The text asks the assistant to search bundled fonts and does not contain shell substitution or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around a relative directory path. There is no executable command, interpolation, or user-controlled command string at this location.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional External Asset Retrieval
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill may ask the assistant to retrieve additional fonts from the network. This is a bounded design workflow risk, not evidence of malware.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicitly requested for font acquisition. The skill also includes many bundled fonts, reducing but not eliminating the need for external downloads.

content.value_statement: Static artwork often looks generic when prompts focus only on layout. This skill builds a visual philosophy first, then turns it into a polished PNG or PDF composition.
actual_capabilities: Creates a written visual philosophy before generating the final artwork.
Guides production of single-page PNG or PDF visual artifacts.
Emphasizes minimal text, strong composition, typography, and spatial design.
Uses bundled font files from the canvas-fonts directory when suitable.
Supports multi-page visual sets when the user requests them.
Adds a refinement pass focused on spacing, containment, and polish.
limitations: It is designed for static artwork, not interactive interfaces or animations.
It may suggest downloading fonts if bundled fonts are not enough.
It does not guarantee brand compliance unless brand rules are provided.
It relies on the host assistant tools to create the actual PNG or PDF file.

use_cases.title: Exhibition Poster Concept
use_cases.description: Create a refined poster direction with a visual philosophy, sparse text, and strong typographic hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Editorial Art Page
use_cases.description: Turn a theme or article concept into a static art page suitable for presentation or publication drafts.

use_cases.title: Moodboard Alternative
use_cases.description: Generate a polished visual artifact that communicates an aesthetic direction without a traditional moodboard.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one visual artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a single-page PNG poster for [topic]. Use minimal text and make the design feel polished and intentional.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Mood Artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PDF visual artifact for [brand or project]. Use these traits: [traits]. Include a short visual philosophy and the final design.

prompt_templates.title: Editorial Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for abstract subject matter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a design-forward editorial art page about [theme]. Make the reference subtle, sophisticated, and mostly visual.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a cohesive set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [number]-page PDF series for [topic]. Keep one visual philosophy, but make each page distinct and refined.

output_examples.input: Create a poster for a small ceramics studio opening.
output_examples.output: A concise visual philosophy followed by a one-page poster with restrained typography, tactile texture, and balanced negative space.

output_examples.input: Make an abstract art page about late-night software work.
output_examples.output: A polished PNG concept using sparse labels, systematic marks, deep contrast, and a subtle technical reference.

output_examples.input: Create a three-page visual series for a jazz archive.
output_examples.output: A cohesive PDF sequence with varied compositions, rhythm-based geometry, minimal text, and consistent typographic treatment.

best_practices: Provide the subject, format, size, and any required text before generation.
State brand colors, forbidden styles, and must-use words if they matter.
Review the first output for spacing, legibility, and whether the visual reference is too obvious.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for dense reports, slide decks, or text-heavy documents.
Do not ask it to copy a living artist, brand campaign, or copyrighted artwork.
Do not rely on external font downloads when your environment blocks network access.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates static visual artwork, usually as a PNG or PDF, supported by a short design philosophy.

faq.question: Can it create multi-page work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for multi-page PDF or PNG series when the user requests more than one page.

faq.question: Does it use bundled fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a canvas-fonts directory and tells the assistant to use different fonts when writing text.

faq.question: Will it access the network?
faq.answer: It may suggest downloading fonts if needed. Use the bundled fonts when network access is not allowed.

faq.question: Is it safe for brand work?
faq.answer: It can support brand work when you provide brand rules, approved text, colors, and style limits.

faq.question: What should users avoid?
faq.answer: Users should avoid requests that copy protected artwork or require large amounts of readable body text.
